sources_audited.note: The Census archive defines 311612 as processing or preserving non-poultry meat from purchased meats and includes assembly cutting and boxed-meat packing. It supports the industry-boundary premise, not the estimated prevalence of the narrowed outsourced lens.

The May 2023 BLS table reports 542,990 jobs and the cited occupation shares, including 59.77% production, 18.43% transportation/material moving, 6.47% installation/maintenance, 4.31% office support, and 33.00% meat-processing workers. The source population is the disclosed broader NAICS 311600.

OSHA includes 311612 within its NAICS 3116 scope, describes mechanization and hazardous equipment, and reports the cited 2022 DART comparison. These facts provide qualitative context only for a and rho, as the packet's proxy treatment acknowledges.

The USDA-hosted research-project description says robotic uptake in American meat and hoofstock facilities is fairly limited and attributes that to expense, single-job designs, high infrastructure requirements, and limited benefits to surrounding line work. It is a project rationale rather than deployment research.

The July 2026 Parsec release describes a February 2026 survey of 1,200 global manufacturing leaders and reports 72% adoption, 10% scaled deployment, 22% active implementation, the cited use cases, and implementation barriers. Its vendor-sponsored, cross-manufacturing population is disclosed.

Cargill documents its February 2024 purchase of two case-ready plants and a long-term retailer partnership producing packaged beef and pork, muscle cuts, and value-added products for grocery-store brands. It demonstrates the model and transfer, but supplies no LMM prevalence or contract-retention measure.

Food Business News reports Kingswood's November 2024 acquisition of Branding Iron, a branded and private-label value-added meat processor with three plants. This is an illustrative transaction, not a transfer-rate or eligibility-prevalence dataset.

The Census infographic reports 51% age 55 or older, 43% age 35-54, and 6% age 34 or younger for 4.1 million responding employer-business owners in data year 2018. It does not measure firms, 311612 owners, or actual succession events.

Baker Tilly reports Capital IQ counts of 165 U.S. food-and-beverage deals in H2 2024, 147 in H1 2024, and 131 in H2 2023, with food products at 67% of H2 closings. The population is much broader than the lens and does not identify qualifying control transfers.

The USDA tables, completed in November 2025, report the cited 2026 and 2031 commercial-production values for beef and pork; their sums reproduce the stated approximately 1.08 ratio. They forecast commodity production, not constant-quality outsourced-processing demand.

The 2015 ERS article explains meat price spreads as gross-margin indicators and states that processing-technology improvements could lower wholesale-to-retail spreads, benefiting producers and/or consumers. It does not measure contract structure or five-year savings retention for eligible processors.

9 CFR 302.1 requires inspection, subject to enumerated exemptions, for establishments preparing livestock-carcass products for commerce as human food. This supports continuing accountable physical preparation, not retention by the same outsourced supplier.

9 CFR 417.2 requires official establishments to conduct hazard analysis and, when relevant hazards are reasonably likely, implement written HACCP plans with controls, records, verification, and responsible-establishment signature. It does not quantify AI implementation or outsourced-operator retention.

caveats: S1 is a 1997 NAICS archive rather than the current classification release. Its quoted 311612 definition supports the packet's boundary premise, but the archival vintage is a metadata weakness.

The a estimate is derived from broader NAICS 311600 occupation shares plus qualitative OSHA context, without a reproducible wage-weighted task map for six-digit LMM contract processors. The PROXY bridge and error directions are disclosed, and even the registered high leaves H below 1, so this does not change the base tier or action.

The rho evidence combines a vendor-sponsored global manufacturing survey with a USDA-hosted robotics project description; neither measures five-year implementation at eligible LMM meat processors. The proxy range is judgmental but honestly labeled, and a downgrade would not change the already MODEL_CONDITIONED action or STRUCTURAL_OUT tier.

S6 and S7 establish that case-ready/private-label operations and transfers exist, but one large-company plant purchase and one three-plant acquisition do not measure the eligible share e. The packet properly labels e an ESTIMATE and identifies firm-level classification as the needed evidence.

The s5 bridge rests on 2018 owner-age data for all responding employer-business owners and broad 2024 food-and-beverage deal counts. Those sources do not measure five-year control-transfer probability for the narrowed lens; the limitation is disclosed and cannot overcome the H weakest link.

The q base is not measured by either cited source: S6 does not disclose pricing or productivity-sharing terms, and S11 only gives a general price-spread mechanism. q is honestly labeled ESTIMATE, so correcting or widening it would not change the base tier or action.

The d5 range applies national beef-and-pork production growth to a narrowed outsourced-processing service basket and may understate insourcing, captive-capacity, mix, and trade uncertainty. The bridge states this mismatch; D is capped at 10 and the record remains STRUCTURAL_OUT because H is the binding factor.

The near-one o range is supported only for the need for an inspected, HACCP-accountable establishment, not continuity of a particular outsourced supplier. The packet explicitly distinguishes those concepts and discloses insourcing and exemption risks; changing o cannot alter the base tier or the estimate-driven action.
summary: Publishable with caveats. Mechanics pass, every primitive-cited source is reachable, and the cited pages support the packet's factual premises. The narrowed recurring outsourced-processing lens is disclosed and coherent, construct roles are not reversed or double-counted, and uncertain values are generally labeled PROXY or ESTIMATE with explicit bridges and change evidence. Several primitives rely on broad, dated, vendor-sponsored, or illustrative evidence rather than direct LMM meat-processor measurements, but under the required materiality test none of those limitations can change the STRUCTURAL_OUT base tier or the already MODEL_CONDITIONED / VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action; H remains below 1 even at the registered high scenario.
